revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=高野神社| 所在地=岡山県津山市高野本郷1012| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|}}| 祭神=高野造祖大神| 創建=不明| 社格=式内社（小）、郷社| 例祭=春祭：3月第3日曜日
夏祭：旧暦6月15日に近い日曜日
秋祭：10月20日に近い日曜日| 本殿=中山造| 画像=[[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|260px]]}} {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} '''高野神社'''（たかのじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[津山市]][[高野本郷]]にある[[神社]]。祭神は高野造祖大神である。主神に[[ウガヤフキアエズ|鵜葺草葺不合命]]、相殿に[[応神天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]。主神・相殿3柱を総称して高野造祖大神と呼ぶ。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]の論社で、市内にはもう一社同名の式内小社論社[[高野神社]]があり、そちらは[[美作国]]二宮となっている。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。 == 概要 == 創建年代は不明である。[[平安時代]]前期の[[貞観]]6年（[[864年]]）に従五位に叙せられたと、初めて文献に登場する。貞観17年（[[875年]]）には正四位下に昇進している。 祭神は本来、鵜葺草葺不合命1柱であった。[[鎌倉時代]]以降、[[武家]]の台頭により[[八幡神]]の応神天皇・神功皇后が加えられた。以後、八幡宮として崇敬されるようになった。この神々により高野の地が開墾され繁栄をもたらしたとして、高野造祖大神と総称し敬慕されている。 現在の本殿は[[江戸時代]]後期の[[文化 (元号)|文化]]9年（1812年）に当地方独自の[[中山造]]にて造営された。 == 画像 == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|一の鳥居 File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|二の鳥居 File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 06.jpg|拝殿 File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|中山造の本殿 ==参考文献== *岡山県高等学校教育研究会社会科部会歴史分科会/編 『新版 岡山県の歴史散歩』 山川出版社 1991年 198ページ *現地説明板 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=03065 高野神社]岡山県神社庁 {{DEFAULTSORT:たかのしんしやつやまたかのほんこう}} [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:津山市]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧郷社]]

{{神社| 名称=高野神社| 所在地=岡山県津山市高野本郷1012| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|}}| 祭神=高野造祖大神| 創建=不明| 社格=式内社（小）、郷社| 例祭=春祭：3月第3日曜日

{{神社| 名称=高野神社| 所在地=岡山県津山市高野本郷1012| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|}}| 祭神=高野造祖大神| 創建=不明| 社格=式内社（小）、郷社| 例祭=春祭：3月第3日曜日

秋祭：10月20日に近い日曜日| 本殿=中山造| 画像=[[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|260px]]}} {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} '''高野神社'''（たかのじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[津山市]][[高野本郷]]にある[[神社]]。祭神は高野造祖大神である。主神に[[ウガヤフキアエズ|鵜葺草葺不合命]]、相殿に[[応神天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]。主神・相殿3柱を総称して高野造祖大神と呼ぶ。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]][[美作国|美作國]][[苫東郡]]高野神社の論社で、市内にはもう一社同名の式内小社論社[[高野神社 (津山市二宮)|高野神社]]があり、そちらは[[美作国]]二宮となっている。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。 == 概要 == 創建年代は不明である。[[平安時代]]前期の[[貞観]]6年（[[864年]]）に従五位に叙せられたと、初めて文献に登場する。貞観17年（[[875年]]）には正四位下に昇進している。 祭神は本来、鵜葺草葺不合命1柱であった。[[鎌倉時代]]以降、[[武家]]の台頭により[[八幡神]]の応神天皇・神功皇后が加えられた。以後、八幡宮として崇敬されるようになった。この神々により高野の地が開墾され繁栄をもたらしたとして、高野造祖大神と総称し敬慕されている。 現在の本殿は[[江戸時代]]後期の[[文化 (元号)|文化]]9年（[[1812年]]）に当地方独自の[[中山造]]にて造営された。 == 画像 == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|一の鳥居 File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|二の鳥居 File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 06.jpg|拝殿 File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|中山造の本殿 ==参考文献== *岡山県高等学校教育研究会社会科部会歴史分科会/編 『新版 岡山県の歴史散歩』 山川出版社 1991年 198ページ *現地説明板 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=03065 高野神社]岡山県神社庁 {{DEFAULTSORT:たかのしんしやつやまたかのほんこう}} [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:津山市]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧郷社]]

{{神社| 名称=高野神社| 所在地=岡山県津山市高野本郷1012| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|}}| 祭神=高野造祖大神| 創建=不明| 社格=式内社（小）、郷社| 例祭=春祭：3月第3日曜日
夏祭：旧暦6月15日に近い日曜日
秋祭：10月20日に近い日曜日| 本殿=中山造| 画像=[[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|260px]]}} {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} '''高野神社'''（たかのじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[津山市]][[高野本郷]]にある[[神社]]。祭神は高野造祖大神である。主神に[[ウガヤフキアエズ|鵜葺草葺不合命]]、相殿に[[応神天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]。主神・相殿3柱を総称して高野造祖大神と呼ぶ。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]][[美作国|美作國]][[苫東郡]]高野神社の論社で、市内にはもう一社同名の式内小社論社[[高野神社 (津山市二宮)|高野神社]]があり、そちらは[[美作国]]二宮となっている。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。 == 概要 == 創建年代は不明である。[[平安時代]]前期の[[貞観]]6年（[[864年]]）に従五位に叙せられたと、初めて文献に登場する。貞観17年（[[875年]]）には正四位下に昇進している。 祭神は本来、鵜葺草葺不合命1柱であった。[[鎌倉時代]]以降、[[武家]]の台頭により[[八幡神]]の応神天皇・神功皇后が加えられた。以後、八幡宮として崇敬されるようになった。この神々により高野の地が開墾され繁栄をもたらしたとして、高野造祖大神と総称し敬慕されている。 現在の本殿は[[江戸時代]]後期の[[文化 (元号)|文化]]9年（[[1812年]]）に当地方独自の[[中山造]]にて造営された。 == 画像 == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|一の鳥居 File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|二の鳥居 File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 06.jpg|拝殿 File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|中山造の本殿 ==参考文献== *岡山県高等学校教育研究会社会科部会歴史分科会/編 『新版 岡山県の歴史散歩』 山川出版社 1991年 198ページ *現地説明板 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=03065 高野神社]岡山県神社庁 {{DEFAULTSORT:たかのしんしやつやまたかのほんこう}} [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:津山市]] [[Category:美作国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧郷社]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 高野神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[津山市]]高野本郷1012 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=高野神社}} |祭神 = 高野造祖大神
（[[ウガヤフキアエズ|鵜葺草葺不合命]]、[[応神天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]の総称） |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）[[論社]]
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[中山造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|一の鳥居]] '''高野神社'''（たかのじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[津山市]][[高野本郷]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]][[論社]]、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == 祭神は以下の3柱。これら神々により当地が開墾され繁栄がもたらされたといい、総称して「'''高野造祖大神'''」と呼ばれている。 ; 主祭神 * '''[[ウガヤフキアエズ|鵜葺草葺不合命]]''' （うがやふきあえずのみこと） ; 相殿神 * [[応神天皇]] * [[神功皇后]] 古くは、祭神は鵜葺草葺不合命1柱であったという境内説明板。。[[鎌倉時代]]以降、[[八幡神]]の応神天皇・神功皇后が加えられた。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建年代は不詳。[[平安時代]]前期の[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]6年（[[864年]]）に従五位に叙せられたと、初めて文献に登場する。貞観17年（[[875年]]）には正五位下に昇進している。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』には「[[美作国]][[苫東郡]] 高野神社」の記載があり、当社はその[[論社]]とされている。もう一社の論社には[[高野神社 (津山市二宮)|高野神社]]（津山市二宮）があり、そちらは[[美作国]][[一宮|二宮]]となっている。二宮の高野神社は美作三大社の一社とされる古社であるが[[苫西郡]]に鎮座しているため、苫東郡に鎮座する当社との間で比定を巡り議論がなされてきたが、結論には至っていない。 [[鎌倉時代]]以降、[[武家]]の台頭により[[八幡神]]の神格が加わり、明治に入るまでの間は「八幡宮」と称されていた。[[永禄]]年間（[[1558年]]-[[1570年]]）には[[尼子晴久]]により再興されたという『岡山県の地名』津山市 高野本郷村項。。 明治維新後、現在の「高野神社」に改称し、[[近代社格制度]]においては[[郷社]]に列した。 === 神階 === 「美作国高野神」に対する奉叙の記録。ただし、当社とするか二宮の[[高野神社 (津山市二宮)|高野神社]]とするかには検討を要する『岡山県の地名』津山市 高野神社項。。 * [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]6年（[[864年]]）8月15日、従五位上 （『[[日本三代実録]]』） * 貞観17年（[[875年]]）3月29日、正五位下 （『日本三代実録』） == 境内 == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|本殿
[[江戸時代]]後期、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]9年（[[1812年]]）に当地方独自の[[中山造]]で造営されたもの。 ファイル:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|拝殿 File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|二の鳥居 == 祭事 == * 春祭 （3月第3日曜日） * 夏祭 （旧暦6月15日に近い日曜日） * 秋祭 （10月20日に近い日曜日） == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[岡山県]][[津山市]]高野本郷1012 ; 交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[因美線]] [[高野駅 (岡山県)|高野駅]] （徒歩約20分） == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 境内説明板 * 『日本歴史地名体系 岡山県の地名』（[[平凡社]]）津山市 高野本郷村項 * 岡山県高等学校教育研究会社会科部会歴史分科会 編『新版 岡山県の歴史散歩』（山川出版社、1991年）198ページ == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=03065 高野神社]（岡山県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかのしんしやつやまたかのほんこう}} [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:津山市]] [[Category:美作国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧郷社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 高野神社 |画像 = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[津山市]]高野本郷1012 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=高野神社}} |祭神 = 高野造祖大神
（[[ウガヤフキアエズ|鵜葺草葺不合命]]、[[応神天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]の総称） |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）[[論社]]
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[中山造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|一の鳥居]] '''高野神社'''（たかのじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[津山市]][[高野本郷]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]][[論社]]、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == 祭神は以下の3柱。これら神々により当地が開墾され繁栄がもたらされたといい、総称して「'''高野造祖大神'''」と呼ばれている。 ; 主祭神 * '''[[ウガヤフキアエズ|鵜葺草葺不合命]]''' （うがやふきあえずのみこと） ; 相殿神 * [[応神天皇]] * [[神功皇后]] 古くは、祭神は鵜葺草葺不合命1柱であったという境内説明板。。[[鎌倉時代]]以降、[[八幡神]]の応神天皇・神功皇后が加えられた。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建年代は不詳。[[平安時代]]前期の[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]6年（[[864年]]）に従五位に叙せられたと、初めて文献に登場する。貞観17年（[[875年]]）には正五位下に昇進している。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』には「[[美作国]][[苫東郡]] 高野神社」の記載があり、当社はその[[論社]]とされている。もう一社の論社には[[高野神社 (津山市二宮)|高野神社]]（津山市二宮）があり、そちらは[[美作国]][[一宮|二宮]]となっている。二宮の高野神社は美作三大社の一社とされる古社であるが[[苫西郡]]に鎮座しているため、苫東郡に鎮座する当社との間で比定を巡り議論がなされてきたが、結論には至っていない。 [[鎌倉時代]]以降、[[武家]]の台頭により[[八幡神]]の神格が加わり、明治に入るまでの間は「八幡宮」と称されていた。[[永禄]]年間（[[1558年]]-[[1570年]]）には[[尼子晴久]]により再興されたという『岡山県の地名』津山市 高野本郷村項。。 明治維新後、現在の「高野神社」に改称し、[[近代社格制度]]においては[[郷社]]に列した。 === 神階 === 「美作国高野神」に対する奉叙の記録。ただし、当社とするか二宮の[[高野神社 (津山市二宮)|高野神社]]とするかには検討を要する『岡山県の地名』津山市 高野神社項。。 * [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]6年（[[864年]]）8月15日、従五位上 （『[[日本三代実録]]』） * 貞観17年（[[875年]]）3月29日、正五位下 （『日本三代実録』） == 境内 == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|本殿
[[江戸時代]]後期、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]9年（[[1812年]]）に当地方独自の[[中山造]]で造営されたもの。 File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|拝殿 File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|二の鳥居 == 祭事 == * 春祭 （3月第3日曜日） * 夏祭 （旧暦6月15日に近い日曜日） * 秋祭 （10月20日に近い日曜日） == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[岡山県]][[津山市]]高野本郷1012 ; 交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[因美線]] [[高野駅 (岡山県)|高野駅]] （徒歩約20分） == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 境内説明板 * 『日本歴史地名体系 岡山県の地名』（[[平凡社]]）津山市 高野本郷村項 * 岡山県高等学校教育研究会社会科部会歴史分科会 編『新版 岡山県の歴史散歩』（山川出版社、1991年）198ページ == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=03065 高野神社]（岡山県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかのしんしやつやまたかのほんこう}} [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:津山市]] [[Category:美作国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧郷社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 高野神社 |画像 = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[津山市]]高野本郷1012 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=高野神社}} |祭神 = 高野造祖大神
（[[ウガヤフキアエズ|鵜葺草葺不合命]]、[[応神天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]の総称） |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）[[論社]]
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[中山造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|一の鳥居]] '''高野神社'''（たかのじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[津山市]][[高野本郷]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]][[論社]]、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == 祭神は以下の3柱。これら神々により当地が開墾され繁栄がもたらされたといい、総称して「'''高野造祖大神'''」と呼ばれている。 '''主祭神''' * '''[[ウガヤフキアエズ|鵜葺草葺不合命]]''' （うがやふきあえずのみこと） '''相殿神''' * [[応神天皇]] * [[神功皇后]] 古くは、祭神は鵜葺草葺不合命1柱であったという境内説明板。。[[鎌倉時代]]以降、[[八幡神]]の応神天皇・神功皇后が加えられた。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建年代は不詳。[[平安時代]]前期の[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]6年（[[864年]]）に従五位に叙せられたと、初めて文献に登場する。貞観17年（[[875年]]）には正五位下に昇進している。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』には「[[美作国]][[苫東郡]] 高野神社」の記載があり、当社はその[[論社]]とされている。もう一社の論社には[[高野神社 (津山市二宮)|高野神社]]（津山市二宮）があり、そちらは[[美作国]][[一宮|二宮]]となっている。二宮の高野神社は美作三大社の一社とされる古社であるが[[苫西郡]]に鎮座しているため、苫東郡に鎮座する当社との間で比定を巡り議論がなされてきたが、結論には至っていない。 [[鎌倉時代]]以降、[[武家]]の台頭により[[八幡神]]の神格が加わり、明治に入るまでの間は「八幡宮」と称されていた。[[永禄]]年間（[[1558年]]-[[1570年]]）には[[尼子晴久]]により再興されたという『岡山県の地名』津山市 高野本郷村項。。 明治維新後、現在の「高野神社」に改称し、[[近代社格制度]]においては[[郷社]]に列した。 === 神階 === 「美作国高野神」に対する奉叙の記録。ただし、当社とするか二宮の[[高野神社 (津山市二宮)|高野神社]]とするかには検討を要する『岡山県の地名』津山市 高野神社項。。 * [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]6年（[[864年]]）8月15日、従五位上 （『[[日本三代実録]]』） * 貞観17年（[[875年]]）3月29日、正五位下 （『日本三代実録』） == 境内 == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|本殿
[[江戸時代]]後期、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]9年（[[1812年]]）に当地方独自の[[中山造]]で造営されたもの。 File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|拝殿 File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|二の鳥居 == 祭事 == * 春祭 （3月第3日曜日） * 夏祭 （旧暦6月15日に近い日曜日） * 秋祭 （10月20日に近い日曜日） == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[岡山県]][[津山市]]高野本郷1012 '''交通アクセス''' * 最寄駅：[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[因美線]] [[高野駅 (岡山県)|高野駅]] （徒歩約20分） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 境内説明板 * 『日本歴史地名体系 岡山県の地名』（[[平凡社]]）津山市 高野本郷村項 * 岡山県高等学校教育研究会社会科部会歴史分科会 編『新版 岡山県の歴史散歩』（山川出版社、1991年）198ページ == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ 高野神社]（岡山県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかのしんしや}} [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:津山市の歴史]] [[Category:美作国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧郷社]] [[Category:津山市の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Takano Shrine |Image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |Location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor Deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor Deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary Deity''' * '''{{ill|Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place Names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === Shrine Ranks === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place Names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place Names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]]

It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place Names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === Shrine Ranks === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place Names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]]

{{Shrine |Name = Takano Shrine |Image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |Location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor Deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor Deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary Deity''' * '''{{ill|Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place Names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === Shrine Ranks === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place Names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place Names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]]

File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place Names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]]

{{Shrine |Name = Takano Shrine |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place Names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === Shrine Ranks === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place Names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place Names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]]

In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place Names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === Shrine Ranks === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place Names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place Names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]]

Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place Names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place Names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place Names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:dd]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]]

During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代type制度}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)-qq]] [[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]]

It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代type制度}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代type制度}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代type制度}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[Category:Shinto shrines in
