Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[en:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|12=draft}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|12=draft}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|12=draft}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu|12=draft}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|12=draft}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|12=draft}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神|12=draft}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄|12=draft}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久|12=draft}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|12=draft}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|12=draft}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線|12=draft}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[en:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|12=draft|12=draft}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|12=draft|12=draft}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft|12=draft}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|12=draft|12=draft}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神|12=draft|12=draft}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄|12=draft|12=draft}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久|12=draft|12=draft}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|12=draft|12=draft}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|12=draft|12=draft}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線|12=draft|12=draft}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[en:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]]

File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線|12=draft|12=draft}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[en:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|12=draft|12=draft}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|12=draft|12=draft}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft|12=draft}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|12=draft|12=draft}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神|12=draft|12=draft}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄|12=draft|12=draft}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久|12=draft|12=draft}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|12=draft|12=draft}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|12=draft|12=draft}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線|12=draft|12=draft}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[en:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]]

Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久|qq=draft|qq=draft}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[en:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]]

In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久|qq=draft|qq=draft}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[en:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久|qq=draft|qq=draft}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine

File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha shrines]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[en:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Former Gō-sha shrines" to "Former Gō-sha"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久|qq=draft|qq=draft}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[en:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]]
