File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Inbi Line|qq=}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[en:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}, and under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern typeing System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman deities|qq=}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku period|qq=}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amago Haruhisa|qq=}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|qq=}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Inbi Line|qq=}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[en:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]]

During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman deities|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman deities|qq=}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku period|ja|永禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku period|qq=}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|ja|尼子晴久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amago Haruhisa|qq=}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|qq=}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kōno Station|ja|高野駅 (岡山県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR Inbi Line|ja|因美線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Inbi Line|qq=}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[en:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deities]]. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku period]] (1558–1570) by [[:en:Amago Haruhisa|Amago Haruhisa]]."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|qq=}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: [[:en:Takano Station|Kōno Station]] on the [[:en:Inbi Line|JR Inbi Line]] (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]]

Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deities]]. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku period]] (1558–1570) by [[:en:Amago Haruhisa|Amago Haruhisa]]."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|qq=}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: [[:en:Takano Station|Kōno Station]] on the [[:en:Inbi Line|JR Inbi Line]] (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]]

Built in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: [[:en:Takano Station|Kōno Station]] on the [[:en:Inbi Line|JR Inbi Line]] (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deities]]. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku period]] (1558–1570) by [[:en:Amago Haruhisa|Amago Haruhisa]]."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|qq=}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: [[:en:Takano Station|Kōno Station]] on the [[:en:Inbi Line|JR Inbi Line]] (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]]

In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deities]]. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku period]] (1558–1570) by [[:en:Amago Haruhisa|Amago Haruhisa]]."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|qq=}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: [[:en:Takano Station|Kōno Station]] on the [[:en:Inbi Line|JR Inbi Line]] (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] {{translated page|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|version=85435316|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deities]]. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deities]]. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku period]] (1558–1570) by [[:en:Amago Haruhisa|Amago Haruhisa]]."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|qq=}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: [[:en:Takano Station|Kōno Station]] on the [[:en:Inbi Line|JR Inbi Line]] (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] {{translated page|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|version=85435316|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Okayama]] [[Category:津山市の建築物]] [[Category:津山市の歴史]]

Built in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: [[:en:Takano Station|Kōno Station]] on the [[:en:Inbi Line|JR Inbi Line]] (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|version=85435316|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Okayama]] [[Category:津山市の建築物]] [[Category:津山市の歴史]] [[Category:美作国の式内社]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deities]]. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku period]] (1558–1570) by [[:en:Amago Haruhisa|Amago Haruhisa]]."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|qq=}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: [[:en:Takano Station|Kōno Station]] on the [[:en:Inbi Line|JR Inbi Line]] (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|version=85435316|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Okayama]] [[Category:津山市の建築物]] [[Category:津山市の歴史]] [[Category:美作国の式内社]]

It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deities]]. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku period]] (1558–1570) by [[:en:Amago Haruhisa|Amago Haruhisa]]."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|qq=}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: [[:en:Takano Station|Kōno Station]] on the [[:en:Inbi Line|JR Inbi Line]] (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|version=85435316|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Okayama]] [[Category:津山市の建築物]] [[Category:津山市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:qq]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deities]]. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku period]] (1558–1570) by [[:en:Amago Haruhisa|Amago Haruhisa]]."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|qq=}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg|280px]] |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|4|39.20|N|134|3|8.17|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deities]]. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku period]] (1558–1570) by [[:en:Amago Haruhisa|Amago Haruhisa]]."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|qq=}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: [[:en:Takano Station|Kōno Station]] on the [[:en:Inbi Line|JR Inbi Line]] (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|version=85435316|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Okayama]] [[Category:津山市の建築物]] [[Category:津山の Municipal History]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]][[Category:qq]]

During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deities]]. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku period]] (1558–1570) by [[:en:Amago Haruhisa|Amago Haruhisa]]."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|qq=}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: [[:en:Takano Station|Kōno Station]] on the [[:en:Inbi Line|JR Inbi Line]] (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:akano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|version=85435316|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Okayama]] [[Category:津山市の建築物]] [[Category:津山の Municipal History]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province]] [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]] [[sn:Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)]]
