[[co:commons:Category:Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)]][[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] [[co:commons:Category:Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)]][[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)]][[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.0775556|N|134.0522694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}|prefecture = {{ill|Tsuyama|WD=Q841125|1=Tsuyama}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|WD=Q11364287|1=Nakayama-zukuri}} }}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|1=Nakayama-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364287}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan|1=Zhenguan}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman deities|1=Hachiman}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku period|1=Eiroku}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|en|Amago Haruhisa|WD=Q3273106|lt=Amago Haruhisa|1=Amago Haruhisa}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|1=Takano Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673131}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Takano Station|en|Takano Station|WD=Q7677184|lt=Kōno Station|1=Takano Station}} on the {{ill|Inbi Line|en|Inbi Line|WD=Q899509|lt=JR Inbi Line|1=Inbi Line}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] {{translated page|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|version=85435316|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11673133}} [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Okayama]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Tsuyama]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Takano Station|en|Takano Station|WD=Q7677184|lt=Kōno Station|1=Takano Station}} on the {{ill|Inbi Line|en|Inbi Line|WD=Q899509|lt=JR Inbi Line|1=Inbi Line}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] {{translated page|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|version=85435316|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11673133}} [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Okayama]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Tsuyama]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)]][[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] [[co:commons:Category:Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)]][[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)]][[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.0775556|N|134.0522694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}|prefecture = {{ill|Tsuyama|WD=Q841125|1=Tsuyama}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|WD=Q11364287|1=Nakayama-zukuri}} }}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|1=Nakayama-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364287}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan|1=Zhenguan}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman deities|1=Hachiman}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku period|1=Eiroku}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|en|Amago Haruhisa|WD=Q3273106|lt=Amago Haruhisa|1=Amago Haruhisa}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|1=Takano Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673131}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Takano Station|en|Takano Station|WD=Q7677184|lt=Kōno Station|1=Takano Station}} on the {{ill|Inbi Line|en|Inbi Line|WD=Q899509|lt=JR Inbi Line|1=Inbi Line}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] {{translated page|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|version=85435316|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11673133}} [[Category:Buildings in Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Okayama]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Tsuyama]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Takano Station|en|Takano Station|WD=Q7677184|lt=Kōno Station|1=Takano Station}} on the {{ill|Inbi Line|en|Inbi Line|WD=Q899509|lt=JR Inbi Line|1=Inbi Line}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] {{translated page|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|version=85435316|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11673133}} [[Category:Buildings in Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Okayama]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Tsuyama]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)]][[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] [[co:commons:Category:Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)]][[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)]][[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.0775556|N|134.0522694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}|prefecture = {{ill|Tsuyama|WD=Q841125|1=Tsuyama}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|WD=Q11364287|1=Nakayama-zukuri}} }}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|1=Nakayama-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364287}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan|1=Zhenguan}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman deities|1=Hachiman}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku period|1=Eiroku}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|en|Amago Haruhisa|WD=Q3273106|lt=Amago Haruhisa|1=Amago Haruhisa}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|1=Takano Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673131}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Takano Station|en|Takano Station|WD=Q7677184|lt=Kōno Station|1=Takano Station}} on the {{ill|Inbi Line|en|Inbi Line|WD=Q899509|lt=JR Inbi Line|1=Inbi Line}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] {{translated page|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|version=85435316|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11673133}} [[Category:Buildings in Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Okayama]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Tsuyama]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|1=Takano Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673131}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Takano Station|en|Takano Station|WD=Q7677184|lt=Kōno Station|1=Takano Station}} on the {{ill|Inbi Line|en|Inbi Line|WD=Q899509|lt=JR Inbi Line|1=Inbi Line}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] {{translated page|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|version=85435316|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11673133}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tsuyama City]] [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Okayama]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Tsuyama]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Buildings and structures in Tsuyama City" to "Buildings and structures in Tsuyama"
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)]][[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] [[co:commons:Category:Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)]][[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)]][[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.0775556|N|134.0522694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}|prefecture = {{ill|Tsuyama|WD=Q841125|1=Tsuyama}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|WD=Q11364287|1=Nakayama-zukuri}} }}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|1=Nakayama-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364287}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan|1=Zhenguan}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman deities|1=Hachiman}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku period|1=Eiroku}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|en|Amago Haruhisa|WD=Q3273106|lt=Amago Haruhisa|1=Amago Haruhisa}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|1=Takano Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673131}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Takano Station|en|Takano Station|WD=Q7677184|lt=Kōno Station|1=Takano Station}} on the {{ill|Inbi Line|en|Inbi Line|WD=Q899509|lt=JR Inbi Line|1=Inbi Line}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] {{translated page|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|version=85435316|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11673133}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tsuyama]] [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Okayama]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Tsuyama]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Takano Station|en|Takano Station|WD=Q7677184|lt=Kōno Station|1=Takano Station}} on the {{ill|Inbi Line|en|Inbi Line|WD=Q899509|lt=JR Inbi Line|1=Inbi Line}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] {{translated page|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|version=85435316|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11673133}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tsuyama]] [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Okayama]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Tsuyama]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)]][[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] [[co:commons:Category:Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)]][[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)]][[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 03.jpg |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.0775556|N|134.0522694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity (Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Empress Jingū) |Sacred Object = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}|prefecture = {{ill|Tsuyama|WD=Q841125|1=Tsuyama}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|WD=Q11364287|1=Nakayama-zukuri}} }}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Shrine = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|1=Nakayama-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364287}} |Alias = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} [[File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Takanohongo]], [[Tsuyama City]], {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities collectively known as the "Takanotsukuri Ancestor deity," representing the development and prosperity of the region. '''Primary deity''' * '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|lt=Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto|d=Ugayafukiaezu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}''' (Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto) '''Associated Deities''' * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} Originally, the shrine enshrined only Ugaya Fukiaezu no Mikoto. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were added to its pantheon.Shrine information board on-site. == History == === Summary === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It first appeared in historical records in the 6th year of {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan|1=Zhenguan}} (864 CE) when it was granted Junior Fifth Rank. In the 17th year of Jōgan (875 CE), it was elevated to Senior Fifth Rank. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, "Takano Shrine in Toma District of Mimasaka Province" is listed. The shrine is a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, and its claim is contested with {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in Ninomiya. While the Ninomiya shrine is part of the "Three Major Shrines of Mimasaka," its location in Tomo District (苫西郡) conflicts with the Toma District (苫東郡) specified in the Engishiki, leaving the debate unresolved. During the Kamakura period, the shrine's deities were supplemented with the {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman deities|1=Hachiman}}. Until the Meiji era, it was referred to as "Hachiman Shrine." It was restored during the {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku period|1=Eiroku}} (1558–1570) by {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|en|Amago Haruhisa|WD=Q3273106|lt=Amago Haruhisa|1=Amago Haruhisa}}."Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was renamed "Takano Shrine" and designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. === types === Historical records documenting offerings to the "Mimasaka Province Takano God." It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|1=Takano Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673131}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Takano Station|en|Takano Station|WD=Q7677184|lt=Kōno Station|1=Takano Station}} on the {{ill|Inbi Line|en|Inbi Line|WD=Q899509|lt=JR Inbi Line|1=Inbi Line}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] {{translated page|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|version=85435316|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11673133}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tsuyama]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Tsuyama]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

It remains uncertain whether they refer to this shrine or {{ill|Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)|1=Takano Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673131}}."Takano Shrine," *Okayama Prefecture Place names*. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE), Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) * Jōgan 17 (875 CE), Senior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) == Grounds == File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 07.jpg|Main Shrine
Built in the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, Bunka 9 (1812 CE) in the unique {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|ja|中山造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-zukuri|qq=}} style of the region. File:Takano-jinja (takanohongou) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Takanohongo Takano jinja 02.jpg|Second Torii == Festivals == * **Spring Festival** – 3rd Sunday in March * **Summer Festival** – Sunday near the 15th day of the 6th lunar month * **Autumn Festival** – Sunday near October 20 == Access == '''Address''' * {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} [[Tsuyama City]] Takanohongo 1012 '''Transportation''' * Nearest Station: {{ill|Takano Station|en|Takano Station|WD=Q7677184|lt=Kōno Station|1=Takano Station}} on the {{ill|Inbi Line|en|Inbi Line|WD=Q899509|lt=JR Inbi Line|1=Inbi Line}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine information board * "Takanohongo Village," *Okayama Prefecture Place names* (Heibonsha). * Okayama Prefecture Historical Research Group, ed., *Revised Okayama Prefecture Historical Walk* (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991), p. 198. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takano-jinja (Takanohongo, Tsuyama)}} * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16996/ Takano Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) [[ja:高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] {{translated page|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|version=85435316|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11673133}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tsuyama]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Tsuyama]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
